title: Reframe `PitNextLap` as a discrete-time hazard model with auxiliary `laps_to_next_pit` supervision
summary: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just an iid binary row classification task.
rationale: This target is naturally a time-to-event problem, not just an iid binary row classification task. Discrete-time survival models are explicitly designed to estimate conditional event probabilities at each time step, and recent F1 pit-stop papers also report gains from jointly modeling next-stop timing and stop/no-stop decisions.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race-Driver` sequence, compute `laps_to_next_pit` causally from the future stop marker in train only. Train either (a) a hazard-style binary model on expanded per-lap records with stint/race history features, or (b) a multi-task model with heads for `PitNextLap` and `laps_to_next_pit`. Blend its OOF probabilities with the current best GBDT rather than replacing it first.
expected_effect: Should improve discrimination near pit windows and reduce the current model’s tendency to treat adjacent laps independently.
risk: Easy to leak future information when building `laps_to_next_pit`; censoring the final observed stint and keeping feature generation strictly causal is mandatory.
